content.summaryText: Bleeding Control and Tourniquet Use in Trauma First Response focuses on ABCDE trauma survey with hemorrhage control. Primary objective: Apply direct pressure effectively.

content.questionText: In Bleeding Control and Tourniquet Use, which action best supports 'Apply direct pressure effectively'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Secure scene safety, identify life threats, and control major bleeding
Focusing on minor injuries before treating life-threatening problems
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: secure scene safety, identify life threats, and control major bleeding. Practical emphasis: Place tourniquet correctly.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when airway compromise, shock signs, uncontrolled bleeding, or reduced GCS.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
